posts.author: yaaramanil_kumar
posts.text: Adjustments in F&O contracts of BANKINDIA on account of extraordinary dividend 2026 — Good summary. A few practical points for traders holding positions: Futures traders — the ₹4.65 downward adjustment on May 29 is not a loss. If you hold equity shares, the dividend received offsets it. If you are only in futures with no shares, your carry-forward price simply reflects the ex-dividend value. Options traders — the strike adjustment is automatic. No need to close and re-enter positions. Your ₹140 CE becomes ₹135.35 CE with the same quantity and expiry. Intrinsic value is preserved. Important for…

posts.author: Shruthi
posts.text: Adjustments in F&O contracts of BANKBARODA on account of extraordinary dividend 2026 — The Board of Directors of Bank of Baroda (BANKBARODA) has declared a dividend of ₹8.50 per equity share, with the ex-dividend date being June 05, 2026. SEBI has prescribed a framework for exchanges to adjust derivative contracts at the time of corporate actions. The exchange has published detailed guidelines on such adjustments. As per this framework, if a company declares a dividend that is equal to or more than 2% of the market value of the underlying security, it is treated as an extraordinary dividend, a…

posts.author: Shruthi
posts.text: Adjustments in F&O contracts of TECHM on account of extraordinary dividend 2026 — Hi Mahesh, apologies for the previous response. If the ex-date for dividends is 3rd July, you will be eligible to receive dividends if you purchase shares on or before 2nd July. In your case, you will be eligible to receive dividends if you purchase it on 2nd July. Also, you will still be eligible for dividends if you sell the shares on the ex-date.

posts.author: Mahesh6
posts.text: Adjustments in F&O contracts of TECHM on account of extraordinary dividend 2026 — Thanks for revised reply

posts.author: Mahesh6
posts.text: Adjustments in F&O contracts of TECHM on account of extraordinary dividend 2026 — Person who purchases on ex date will be eligible for dividend. Person who sells on ex date will also be eligible. Doesn’t seem correct.

posts.author: Shruthi
posts.text: Adjustments in F&O contracts of TECHM on account of extraordinary dividend 2026 — Yes, you will qualify for corporate action benefits if you purchase shares before the exdate. Also, you will still be eligible for corporate action benefits even if you sell the shares on the ex-date/record date.

posts.author: Mahesh6
posts.text: Adjustments in F&O contracts of TECHM on account of extraordinary dividend 2026 — Ex dividend date is 3rd july. Suppose 2nd july is not a holiday. Shall I get dividend if I buy shares in cash market CNC on 2nd july and sell on 3rd july?
